scenario_title: delete_rrset for an absent type after successful prereq
test_rationale: This scenario isolates the underspecified behavior with a single UPDATE: the prerequisite on helper.campus.edu. confirms the update is otherwise admissible, and the operation deletes a TXT RRset that does not exist at absent.campus.edu. Because the post-state is unchanged either way, the only observable divergence is the UPDATE reply code, which is exactly the gap left open by RFC 2136 for no-op RRset deletion.

group_1.behavior: Treat delete_rrset of an absent RRset as a successful no-op after the prerequisite passes.

group_2.behavior: Treat delete_rrset of an absent RRset as a semantic error even though prerequisites passed.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2136 §3.4.3 defines delete_rrset as deleting all RRs of a type at a name, but it does not clearly require a specific reply code when that RRset is already absent. As a result, implementations may differ between NOERROR for a no-op delete and NXRRSET to report the missing RRset, while RFC 2181 §5.2 does not resolve the update semantic.
